AgentCard.description: A2A-inspired identity record for an endpoint or agent.

description.description: A2A-style one-line summary of what this agent does (discovery / observability).

id.description: Unique, stable for the lifetime of this connection.

kind.description: 'agent' (participates in coordination) or a plain 'endpoint' (logger, dashboard…).

protocolVersion.description: Wire-contract version this participant speaks (the SPEC.md version, `"0.2"` today). A change signal, not negotiation: v0 has none, but a peer can detect a mismatch instead of silently misreading a future envelope. Omitted ⇒ assume the v0.x line.

role.description: Cotal addition: the role this participant plays (planner, reviewer, …).

tags.description: Cotal: free-form "what it can do" tags (A2A skill-tags, flattened) — discovery only.

ChannelConfig.description: Channel registry entry — channel-global config, stored in the per-space channels KV (one entry per channel; the space-wide default lives under  {@link  CHANNEL_DEFAULTS_KEY  } ). Shared across every peer, not a per-subscriber choice. `description`/`instructions` reach the model, so this is a prompt-injection surface: writes are privileged and both text fields are length-bounded at the write path (see channels.ts).

instructions.description: Longer "how to use it" — surfaced to joiners as advisory, attributed data.

replay.description: Override the space default for history replay-on-join.

replayWindow.description: How far back a joiner's backfill reaches — a duration like `"24h"`, `"30m"`, `"7d"`. Maps to a native Direct-Get `start_time` (now − window). Unset + `replay` ⇒ the full retained window; ignored when replay is off.

ChannelDefaults.description: Space-wide channel defaults, stored under  {@link  CHANNEL_DEFAULTS_KEY  } .

CotalMessage.description: A message on the mesh (chat / direct message for now; extensible to other families).

channel.description: Channel name — multicast (broadcast to everyone on the channel).

mentions.description: Lowercased peer names called out within a `channel` message — a priority/wake hint, not a routing target: the message still multicasts to the whole channel. Omitted when empty.

replyTo.description: Id of the message being replied to.

to.description: Instance id — unicast (direct to one specific endpoint).

toService.description: Service / role — anycast (any one instance of the service receives it).

EndpointKind.description:
Cotal wire types (v0).

These are the shapes that travel on the mesh. They are intentionally A2A-inspired (AgentCard / Message / Part) but transport-agnostic. This file IS part of the "wire contract" — treat changes here as protocol changes.

Presence.description: Live presence record. Stored in the space's KV bucket under key = card.id.

ts.description: Epoch ms of the last heartbeat.

PresenceStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a participant.
- `idle`: connected, no active task
- `waiting`: blocked — awaiting input, approval, or a peer
- `working`: actively executing a task / in a turn
- `offline`: disconnected or heartbeat lapsed (derived by observers, not self-set while live)
